Barry Callebaut Target Cut to CHF900 From CHF1,415 by Barclays Capital

'Good Chance' of a U.K. Trade Deal, JD Vance Says
data.description:
By Angus Berwick

There is "a good chance" the U.S. can strike a trade deal with the U.K, Vice President JD Vance said in an interview published Tuesday.

Vance told news website UnHerd the Trump administration is working hard with the U.K. government on a deal. The U.S. imposed baseline tariffs of 10% on the U.K. on April 5.

"The President really loves the United Kingdom. He loved the Queen. He admires and loves the King. It is a very important relationship," Vance said. "I think there's a good chance that, yes, we'll come to a great agreement that's in the best interest of both countries."

It will be harder for other European countries to strike trade deals, Vance said, highlighting Germany as a country that exported heavily to the U.S. The share of German exports to the U.S. hit its highest level last year since 2002, data indicated Monday.

Asked about Vance's comments, the British trade and business minister, Sarah Jones, told the BBC talks were ongoing and the U.K. was in a "good position."

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Microsoft Signs Carbon Removal Deal With AtmosClear
data.description:
Microsoft has signed a contract with AtmosClear BR, part of Fidelis, for 6.75 million metric tons of engineered carbon removal over 15 years from bioenergy carbon capture and storage, AtmosClear BR said Tuesday.

The agreement marks the world's largest deal for permanent carbon removal to date, according to the company.

AtmosClear is developing a carbon capture facility at the Port of Greater Baton Rouge, Louisiana, that will convert sustainable materials like sugarcane bagasse and forest trimmings into clean energy while capturing 680,000 metric tons of biogenic carbon dioxide annually.

Construction is scheduled to begin in 2026, with operations set to start in 2029, the company said.

data.title: These Stocks Are Moving the Most Today: Boeing, Tesla, Netflix, J&J, Bank of America, Allegro MicroSystems, and More
data.description:
By Joe Woelfel

Stock futures suggested a flat start to trading Tuesday with investors trying to make sense of mixed trade policy signals from the Trump administration.

These stocks were poised to make moves Tuesday:

Boeing was down 3.3% after a Bloomberg report said China ordered its airlines not to take any further deliveries of the company's jets as part of trade war between Washington and Beijing.

Tesla rose 0.2% to $252.72. The stock chart of the electric-vehicle maker on Monday formed a death cross, a trading term for when a shorter-term moving average crosses over a longer-term moving average. In this instance, the 50-day moving average moved below the 200-day moving average. A death cross often is a sign that a stock is losing momentum.

Netflix aims to achieve a $1 trillion market capitalization and double its revenue to $39 billion by 2030, according to The Wall Street Journal, which was told about the forecasts from people who attended the streaming company's annual business review meeting last month. Netflix also set a goal of tripling its operating income by 2030 from $10 billion last year, one of the people told the Journal. Netflix shares were rising 2.9% in premarket trading. The company is scheduled to report quarterly earnings on Thursday.

Palantir Technologies rose 1.5% in premarket trading. The data-analytics company closed up 4.6% on Tuesday after the North Atlantic Treaty Organization said it would be using the company's artificial intelligence-enabled software to modernize its military capabilities. William Blair analyst Louie DiPalma said the agreement has "broader geopolitical significance than just another win for Palantir" amid Wall Street concerns that Europe wants to rely less on American defense contractors.

Shares of Apple were up 0.3% in the premarket session. The stock rose 2.2% on Monday following President Donald Trump's rollback of certain tariffs for the tech industry. Apple remains down almost 10% since the president's tariffs announcement on April 2.

Allegro MicroSystems was falling 12% to $19.36 after ON Semiconductor withdrew its proposal to acquire the chip maker for $35.10 a share. In a statement, ON Semiconductor said it determined there was "no actionable path forward." Allegro spurned ON Semiconductor's last proposal in early March that had an implied equity value of $6.9 billion. On Semi rose 0.5%.

MP Materials, the largest rare-earths producer in the Western Hemisphere, was up 5.4% in premarket trading after soaring 22% on Monday. The stock jumped after Beijing halted exports of the metals, which are critical for electric vehicles, smartphones, and a host of other technologies.

Applied Digital, the digital infrastructure company, reported a fiscal third-quarter loss of 8 cents a share, narrower than analysts' estimates that called for a loss of 10 cents. Revenue jumped 22% to $52.9 million but missed expectations of $64.8 million. The stock fell 12% in premarket trading.

Earnings reports are expected Tuesday from Bank of America, Citigroup, Johnson & Johnson, PNC Financial, United Airlines, Interactive Brokers, and J.B. Hunt Transport Services.

Analysts expect Bank of America to post first-quarter earnings of 82 cents a share. Revenue was forecast at $26.97 billion. Shares of the bank were up 0.9% in premarket trading. Citigroup, meanwhile, gained 1.2% ahead of earnings Tuesday from the New York bank.

Healthcare giant Johnson & Johnson slipped 0.2% in premarket trading ahead of its first-quarter earnings report. Wall Street expects J&J to post adjusted earnings in the period of $2.69 a share on revenue of $21.56 billion.

Write to Joe Woelfel at joseph.woelfel@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Ideas Worth Printing: eufyMake Unveils World's First Personal 3D-Texture UV Printer
data.description:
BELLVUE,WASHINGTON, April 15, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — eufyMake, the new creative tool brand from Anker Innovations, today announced the brand's flagship product, the UV Printer E1. The E1 is the industry's first 3D-texture UV Printer designed for personal use with true professional-grade capabilities. The E1 is currently available for pre-order on eufyMake.com. Between now and April 28, 2025 , early-bird backers can make a $50 deposit to unlock discounts of up to $800. The E1 will start a Kickstarter crowdfunding campaign on April 29. 

“We want the E1 to shift the UV printing experience away from factory walls, and into homes, studios, small businesses and art markets around the world," said Frank Zhu, General Manager of eufy. "The E1 is designed to print full-color textures on a vast variety of surfaces, shapes and sizes. The compact design, wide assortment of printing accessories and simple user experience makes our first product from eufyMake equally accessible to professional designers, small business owners, first-time UV printer users, creative 'makers' and everyone in between."

 Highlighted Features and Capabilities

  • Innovative Amass3D™️ Technology - Harness the brilliance of Amass3D™️ technology, which creates rich, tactile 3D textures up to 5mm high. Print on nearly any surface imaginable, whether it’s flat panels, rotational surfaces, or roll-to-film materials.
  • 100% Accurate True Colors on Multi Materials - The ColorMaestro™️ system delivers millions of precise, vibrant hues across a stunning array of surfaces—wood, metal, glass, leather, acrylic, etc.
  • One-Stop Intelligent Workflow - Simplicity meets sophistication with AI-powered design tools, specialized software, and an intuitive interface. Featuring 20,000+ templates and innovative AI tools, combined with precise auto-positioning powered by dual laser and SnapShot Camera, the E1 streamlines every step of your creation, ensuring top-notch results.
  • 3-in-1 Modular Design - The UV Printer E1’s sleek form factor features a major innovation : its compact 3-in-1 modular design combines a rotary machine, UV DTF laminating machine, and flatbed —all seamlessly integrated to save space without skimping on capability. Its size is 90% smaller than a traditional UV printer and perfect for cozy home studios or shared creative spaces.
  • Hassle-Free Maintenance - With the JetClean™️system designed to prevent clogs automatically, your printer stays at peak performance with minimal effort. Its certification by Greenguard, ClimatePartner, and TÜV means you can create with peace of mind—safe for both your family and the planet.

UV Printer E1 Accessories

  • Ink and Cleaning Cartrtridge Kit for UV Printer E1
    • UV Ink Cartrtridge (C) 100 ml
    • UV Ink Cartrtridge (M) 100 ml
    • UV Ink Cartrtridge (Y) 100 ml
    • UV Ink Cartrtridge (K) 100 ml
    • UV Ink Cartrtridge (W) 100 ml
    • UV Ink Cartrtridge (G) 100 ml
    • UV Printer Cleaning Cartrtridge 380 ml
  • Rotary Printing Attachment for UV Printer E1 This attachment allows users to directly print 3D-texture into cylinders, cones and mugs.
  • UV DTF Laminating MachineThis machine enables users to create stickers on their own just at home or in studio.

Pricing and Availability

Starting today until April 28, 2025, early adopters can pre-order via eufymake.com with a $50 deposit to unlock exclusive super early bird discounts worth up to $800.

Official Website Deposit Holders (limited quantity) can purchase at only $1,499 USD for a standard bundle including eufymake UV Printer E1 + Complimentary UV Ink Bundle (worth $299 ).

The eufyMake UV Printer E1 will officially kick off its Kickstarter campaign on April 29, 2025 with standard bundle priced at $1,899 USD,a remarkably approachable cost compared to traditional UV printing systems.

There will be a limited super early bundle priced at $ 1,699 USD available on a first-come, first-served basis at the start of the crowdfunding campaign.

About Anker Innovations

Anker Innovations is a global leader in charging and home energy storage technology and a developer of unique, consumer electronic products that support premium audio, mobile entertainment and the emerging smart home space. This innovation is being led by its key brands: Anker, Anker SOLIX, AnkerWork, eufy, Nebula, and Soundcore. More information on Anker Innovations and its various brands can be found at anker.com. 

About eufyMake

eufyMake is the newest addition to the eufy brand family of smart home products and services. The eufyMake "Make it Real" mission is to develop powerful, easy-to-use tools that seamlessly integrate artistic creativity into everyday life and empower "makers" to transform their imagination into reality. More information is available at eufymake.com.

data.title: Lowe's Strikes $1.33 Billion Deal to Buy Artisan Design From Sterling Group
data.description:
Lowe's agreed to acquire interior finishes solutions provider Artisan Design Group from private equity firm Sterling Group in a deal worth about $1.33 billion, as the home improvement retailer looks to expand offerings for its professional customer base.

The transaction, which requires approval from regulators, is expected to be completed in the second quarter, the companies said in separate late Monday statements. Lowe's plans to fund the acquisition with cash on hand.

The home improvement retailer expects the deal to expand its Pro offering into a new distribution channel. "The acquisition of (Artisan Design) allows us to build on our momentum with Pro planned spend and is expected to expand our total addressable market by approximately $50 billion," Lowe's Chief Executive Marvin Ellison said.

Artisan Design, which operates 132 distribution, design and service facilities across 18 states, generated revenue of roughly $1.8 billion in fiscal 2024, according to Lowe's. The firm is headquartered in Dallas, Texas, with a network of more than 3,200 specialized installers, the companies said.

"We are thrilled for (Artisan Design) to join forces with Lowe's," Artisan Design CEO Steve Margolius said. "Our leading position in flooring, cabinets and countertops, combined with Lowe's scale and category breadth, will allow us to continue on our growth trajectory while providing an even more differentiated and comprehensive offering to the builders and property managers we serve today."

The acquisition is in line with Lowe's recent focus on growing its overall professional customer business, which has continued to benefit the retailer with its outperformance versus the do-it-yourself category, Truist Securities said in a late Monday client note. The brokerage expects the expansion of Lowe's total addressable market to provide it with further growth runway over time.

During a fiscal fourth-quarter earnings call in February, Ellison said the company recorded a high-single-digit comparable sales gain in its professional business for the second consecutive quarter.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson 1Q Net $11B >JNJ
Johnson & Johnson 1Q International Sales $9.59B >JNJ
Johnson & Johnson 1Q EPS $4.54 >JNJ
Johnson & Johnson 1Q Adj EPS $2.77 >JNJ
J&J Increases Outlook After Beating 1Q Expectations
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Johnson & Johnson raised its full-year outlook as it logged better-than-expected profit and revenue in the first quarter.

The healthcare-products company on Tuesday said its higher outlook reflects its completed acquisition of mental-illness drug developer Intra-Cellular Therapies. The roughly $15 billion deal in part added Caplyta, a pill that treats bipolar depression and schizophrenia, to J&J's portfolio.

The New Brunswick, N.J., company now expects sales of up to $91.8 billion in 2025, compared with a prior view of up to $90 billion. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected sales of $90.3 billion for the year.

The company backed its adjusted per-share earnings outlook of $10.50 to $10.70, ahead of the $10.46 that analysts modeled.

J&J said its forecast includes tariff costs, dilution from its acquisition of Intra-Cellular and updated foreign exchange rates.

The outlook came as the company posted a profit of $11 billion, or $4.54 a share, in the first quarter, compared with a profit of $3.26 billion, or $1.34 a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted per-share earnings of $2.77 came in ahead of the $2.58 that analysts polled by FactSet expected.

Revenue increased 2.4% to $21.89 billion, topping the $21.56 billion that analysts were looking for.

J&J's pharmaceutical business posted a 2.3% gain, boosted in part by higher sales of cancer drug Darzalex and depression treatment Spravato. Growth was partially offset by falling sales of Stelara, a treatment for psoriasis and Crohn's disease, patents for which have started to expire.

Medical-device sales rose 2.5%. The company attributed the segment's higher sales to Abiomed, its subsidiary that manufactures cardiovascular medical implant devices, as well as increased sales of wound closure products.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: European Gas Price Rises as Market Remains TightMarket Talk
data.description: European natural-gas prices rise in midday trade, with the benchmark Dutch TTF contract up 2.7% at 35.42 euros a megawatt hour. "For now, fundamentals in the European gas market remain supportive," ING analysts say. The bloc ended the 2024-25 heating season with storage levels a little under 34% full, below the five-year average of 45%. "The region will need to see the strongest net injections since 2022. We may need to see more flexibility on these targets, something that member countries are discussing." Prices have been hit by broader risk-off moves in the market last week, and are still around 16% lower on the month. However, analysts say the market is still set to be tight throughout the year and any downward revision to forecast will require a tangible demand hit. ING currently forecasts TTF at 44 euros a megawatt hour. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: INTERVIEW - Bulgaria's Check Point Care seeks 8.5 mln euro investment
data.description:
SOFIA (Bulgaria), April 15 (SeeNews) - Bulgaria-based Check Point Care, a solution provider for remote continuous patient health management, said it is in a fund raising phase targeting an 8.5 million euro ($9.6 million) investment, aiming to expand its reach and help reduce preventable deaths globally.

"We have a pending investment of 8.5 million euro from the European Innovation Council (EIC) for which we would need a matching investment of the same amount. The overall investment would be 17 million euro and we are looking for a lead investor," Slovenian businesswoman Kaja Vidic, a co-owner and chief business development officer at Check Point Care, told SeeNews on the sidelines of the EIC Summit held in Brussels earlier this month.

EIC is the European Union’s flagship innovation programme to identify, develop and scale up breakthrough technologies and game changing innovations.

Check Point Care has been awarded a grant of 2.5 million euro by the EIC Accelerator, a funding programme for support to start-ups and SMEs for its solution which is already saving lives.

"My talk on the main plenary stage at EIC Summit was to expose the importance of high quality and quantity data for medical decision support, resolving lack of medical staff worldwide and healthcare accessibility," Vidic said.

Check Point Care is offering comprehensive clinical data in its decision support tool that enables early detection and prevention of health deterioration, regardless of time or location. It has set up the first of its kind 24/7 remote observation medical centre that, thanks to a wearable device and the use of artificial intelligence (AI), constantly monitors and manages healthcare delivery for 100,000 acute patients in seven countries across Europe and the Middle East.

Vidic pointed out that the number refers to low and highly acute patients, as this differentiates them on the market, since monitoring significantly acute patients is different to the one for relatively healthy ones.

The solution is being applied for pre and post-surgical patient monitoring in cardiology, oncology, neurology and emergency care, in hospital setting and at home, in national screening programmes, chronic patient population programmes and diagnostics.

"We can observe the whole patient journey from medium care to respective hospital department and then patients at home who are at high risk," Vidic explained.

The company has 41 employees, with nurses and doctors in its medical centre in Kazanlak, southern Bulgaria, as well as IT and technical specialists. Now it aspires to hire more people with its business development team, for sales, marketing and PR, and for further AI development. In terms of expansion, the company is going to widen its reach through distribution partners.

"Our plan is to expand initially within Europe and the Middle East, because we already have very good connections there, as we are working directly with the Saudi ministry of health," Vidic said.

The company expects a revenue of 2.5 million euro in 2025, up 70% on the year, accelerating from a 50% rise in sales in 2024. Vidic believes that the revenue growth will be supported by new distribution partnerships in the seven countries in which it is already operating – the Netherlands, the UK, Germany, Sweden, Bulgaria, Serbia, and Slovenia.

"We are already positive and growing," Vidic said.

The potential new outside investor or investors will have a minority stake of up to one-third of the capital of the company, in line with the rules for EIC financing.

The EIC was set up in March 2021 under the EU Horizon Europe programme. It has a budget of over 10 billion euro between 2021 and 2027 to support game changing innovations. So far, the EIC has backed more than 630 companies and over 450 research projects.

The EIC's investment arm, the EIC Fund, has reached 1.0 billion euro in actual investments in deep tech start-ups, leveraging over four times as much in co-investments. This makes the EIC Fund the largest and the most active deep tech co-investment fund in Europe. To further boost access of breakthrough EIC companies to venture capital, the European Commission has recently launched the EIC trusted investors network, which comprises 111 members, collectively managing over 336 billion euro in assets.

($ = 0.882 euro)

data.title: J&J beats Wall Street quarterly sales and profit estimates on cancer drug sales
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson on Tuesday reported first-quarter revenue and profit above Wall Street estimates, driven again by strong sales of its cancer treatments including multiple myeloma medicine Darzalex.

J&J's quarterly sales stood at $21.89 billion, up 2.4% from a year ago and above analysts' expectations of $21.56 billion, according to LSEG data.

On an adjusted basis, the company earned $2.77 per share in the quarter, 2.2% higher than the previous year and above analysts' estimates of $2.59 per share.

Quarterly sales for the company's innovative medicines unit rose 2.3% to $13.87 billion, beating analyst expectations of $13.43 billion, while quarterly medtech revenue stood at $8.02 billion, up 2.5% on the previous year but below Wall Street estimates of $8.17 billion.

J&J Chief Financial Officer Joe Wolk said in an interview that the company still expected, as indicated in its January guidance, that its medtech business would perform better in the second half of the year.

Innovative medicine revenue was buttressed by a 20% increase in Darzalex sales on the previous year. Darzalex, a blood cancer therapy launched in 2015, brought in first-quarter sales of $3.24 billion, compared to analysts' expectations of $3.05 billion.

The company's cancer cell therapy Carvykti, which it makes in partnership with Legend Biotech , brought in sales of $369 million, above estimates of $324 million.

Quarterly sales of J&J's cancer drugs rose nearly 18% worldwide to $5.68 billion.

Sales of the drugmaker's blockbuster psoriasis treatment Stelara fell more than 33% to $1.63 billion in the first quarter, although beating estimates of $1.42 billion, according to LSEG data.

Close copies of Stelara launched in Europe, Canada and few other markets last year, while biosimilar rivals in the U.S. launched this year.

J&J also raised its annual sales forecast by $700 million at the midpoint while lowering its profit outlook, saying it reflects the impact of tariffs imposed by the Trump administration and dilution from its $14.6 billion deal to buy neurological drugmaker Intra-Cellular.

The company now expects sales of $91.6 billion to $92.4 billion, compared to its previous forecast of $90.9 billion and $91.7 billion, driven by its purchase of Intra-Cellular's top schizophrenia drug Caplyta.

J&J expects to earn $10.50 to $10.70 per share on an adjusted basis compared to its previous guidance of $10.75 to $10.95.

Wolk said the profit adjustment takes account of around $400 million that is expected to be incurred in J&J's medtech business due to tariffs currently in place, including those on China and Mexico.

"That is more of a second quarter (and) forward event ... and covers all the programs in place today even if paused," he said.

data.title: Rape used systematically as a weapon of war in Sudan, UN agency warns
data.description:
Rape is being used systematically as a weapon of war in Sudan, a UN agency warned on Tuesday, as the conflict marks its second year.

"We have seen a 288% increase in demand for life saving support for rape and sexual violence survivors. We are beginning to see the systematic use of rape and sexual violence as a weapon of war," Anna Mutavati, the regional director of UN Women, told reporters in Geneva via video link from Port Sudan.

"Women's bodies have turned into a battleground," she said, without saying which side in Sudan's war was responsible.

The war between Sudan's army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces erupted in April 2023, shattering hopes for a transition to civilian rule.

The conflict has since displaced millions and devastated regions like Darfur, where the RSF is fighting to maintain its stronghold amid army advances in Khartoum.

"This is just the tip of the iceberg, not everyone is coming forward because there is shame and victim blaming that is attached to every woman that has been raped and gang raped," she said.

A U.N. fact-finding mission last year described levels of sexual abuse, including child rape, as "staggering". The majority of known cases were perpetrated by the RSF and its allies, the mission said, noting it was more difficult to report in army-controlled territories.

A representative of another U.N. agency said on Tuesday he had met women in Khartoum who told him they had been sexually assaulted in front of their injured husbands and screaming children.

"It is the first time in my life I have seen women who have been abused to that extent," said Mohamed Refaat, head of the International Organization for Migration's mission in Sudan.

Britain is co-hosting a London conference on Tuesday, aiming to improve coordination of the international response to the crisis. Sudan's foreign minister has criticised the presence of the UAE and Kenya at the talks and said Sudan should have been invited.

data.title: Alcazar, IFC in funding deal for 400 MW wind farm in North Macedonia
data.description:
SKOPJE (N. Macedonia), April 15 (SeeNews) - Luxembourg-based renewable energy investor Alcazar Energy Partners (AEP), said on Tuesday it has signed a development support agreement with the International Finance Corporation (IFC), a member of the World Bank Group, for the construction of a 400 MW wind farm in North Macedonia.

The agreement gives AEP access to development funding for the first phase of the Stip wind farm, which is scheduled to begin later in 2025, the investor said in a press release.

The 500 million euro ($567.1 million) project is set to power over 100,000 households and cut up to 690,000 tonnes of CO2 emissions annually.

The Stip wind farm is AEP’s first project in North Macedonia and aligns with its strategy to grow across the Western Balkans, where it has secured a 1.5 GW pipeline of greenfield wind and solar assets to advance national energy goals and global climate targets, the company said.

In 2023, AEP acquired the rights for a 119 MW wind farm project in Montenegro, valued at approximately $200 million. Last year, the investor also agreed to buy rights for the development, construction, and operation of Project Celzijus 1, a 200 MW onshore wind power project east of Belgrade, alongside a 768 MW pipeline of wind and solar projects in Serbia.

($ = 0.882 euro)

data.title: London Stock Exchange Group's Growth Prospects Look PositiveMarket Talk
data.description: London Stock Exchange Group's growth profile looks attractive, Deutsche Bank's Benjamin Goy writes in a note. The stock-exchange and financial-information company's revenues are largely recurring, the analyst says. The company has some pricing power, supported by an improving data & analytics product. Additionally, transaction-based revenues benefit from structural growth drivers, amplified by current market volatility, he adds. Therefore, the German bank expects more than 7% revenue growth going forward. DB upgrades its stock recommendation to buy from hold and its target price to 12,600 pence from 11,500 pence. Shares are up 1.6% at 11,255 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

data.title: Inception Growth Acquisition Limited Announces Additional Contribution to Trust Account to Extend Business Combination Period
data.description:
New York, April 15, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Inception Growth Acquisition Limited (NASDAQ: IGTA, the “Company”), a publicly traded special purpose acquisition company, announced today that on April 10, 2025, the Company deposited $11,199.60 into the Company’s trust account (the “Trust Account”)  in order to extend the period of time the Company has to complete a business combination for an additional one (1) month period, from April 13, 2025 to May 13, 2025. The purpose of the extension is to provide additional time for the Company to complete a business combination.

About Inception Growth Acquisition Limited

Inception Growth Acquisition Limited is a blank check company incorporated under the laws of Delaware whose business purpose is to effect a merger, capital stock exchange, asset acquisition, stock purchase, reorganization, or similar business combination with one or more businesses or entities. 

Forward Looking Statements

This press release includes forward-looking statements that involve risks and uncertainties. Forward-looking statements are statements that are not historical facts. Such forward-looking statements, including the successful consummation of the Company’s initial public offering, are subject to risks and uncertainties, which could cause actual results to differ from the forward-looking statements. data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1015 GMT - London Stock Exchange Group's growth profile looks attractive, Deutsche Bank's Benjamin Goy writes in a note. The stock-exchange and financial-information company's revenues are largely recurring, the analyst says. The company has some pricing power, supported by an improving data & analytics product. Additionally, transaction-based revenues benefit from structural growth drivers, amplified by current market volatility, he adds. Therefore, the German bank expects more than 7% revenue growth going forward. DB upgrades its stock recommendation to buy from hold and its target price to 12,600 pence from 11,500 pence. Shares are up 1.6% at 11,255 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

1011 GMT - LVMH's results will likely set a negative tone for the 1Q reporting season, Citi analysts Thomas Chauvet and Mahesh Mohankumar write in a note. The performance of the French luxury group, considered a sector bellwether, was overall below the most conservative buy-side expectations, Citi says. With highly elevated economic uncertainty, both in the key U.S. market and globally, it is difficult to build a credible scenario of sequential revenue improvement in the coming two quarters for LVMH and the luxury sector, the analysts say. Consensus expectations for full-year sales and EBIT could be reduced by a low-single-digit and high-single-digit percentage, respectively, they say. Shares trade 7.5% lower at 490.50 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0955 GMT - Publicis delivered a solid first-quarter update that should limit cuts to consensus estimates driven by economic concerns at least for this year, UBS analysts say in a research note. The French advertising group said net revenue grew 4.9% organically in the first quarter, topping expectations of 4.5%. It still expects organic growth of 4% to 5% for the year, with a second-quarter performance within that range. Investors should react positively to the reaffirmation of the 2025 guidance and the new outlook for the second quarter, the analysts say. UBS expects Publicis' second-quarter organic growth to be 4.5%. Shares rise 3.1% to 86.60 euros. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0955 GMT - A U.S. investigation into pharmaceutical imports and potential tariffs in the coming months will likely limit clarity from pharma companies during their 1Q results, Barclays analysts say in a note. The investigation, initiated April 1, aims to assess the national security implications of pharmaceutical imports, including finished generics, nongenerics and active pharmaceutical ingredients, and has to be finalized in around nine months. "With Pharma having been excluded from the initial reciprocal tariffs and it being our house view that some sort of Commerce Department investigation being required for these sectoral tariffs, this isn't too surprising but at least provides some timing framing," they say. Barclays analysts estimate that AstraZeneca, Roche and Grifols appear most insulated, whereas ​GSK and Sandoz look most exposed to a potential tariff risk. (helena.smolak@wsj.com)

0952 GMT - LVMH's sales miss confirms a tough start to the year, Jefferies analysts write in a note. The luxury conglomerate of French billionaire Bernard Arnault logged sales of 20.31 billion euros for the first quarter, missing Visible Alpha consensus of 21.21 billion euros. Analysts had forecast a challenging start to 2025 due to softening trends in the U.S. after the pickup in the prior quarter. To mitigate the potential hit from U.S. tariffs, LVMH could consider price hikes and an increase in production in its facilities in Texas. Looking ahead, the uncertainty around consumers' willingness to spend at the higher price points in the U.S. and Europe as well as the extent to which Chinese consumers will rebound, will be critical, Jefferies says. Shares fall 7%. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0952 GMT - The market is now better prepared for any potential ADR delistings, Julius Baer analysts write in an email. While the market generally assumes that trade tensions between the U.S. and China have peaked, there is still a possibility of Chinese American Depository Receipts being delisted, they add. More Chinese companies have returned to Hong Kong for secondary or dual primary listing compared with that four years ago, they say. Converting ADRs to Hong Kong shares remains a sensible option for investors looking to mitigate the risk of potential delistings, they say. The analysts continue to expect consolidation or correction pressure throughout the second quarter. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0950 GMT - Halfords' business changes are underway, and the company is yielding strong benefits from its strategy, AJ Bell investment director Russ Mould says in a note. The U.K. motoring-and-cycling products provider's integrated service, Fusion, creates cohesion between its retail outlets and its car repair garages. These links are boosting revenue and increasing business resilience given that most people need to keep their cars working, he says. The Fusion segment also provides a solid platform for incoming CEO Henry Birch, who is replacing Graham Stapleton after a seven-year stint, Mould adds. Additionally, the company has found ways to mitigate the impact of higher costs associated with the U.K.'s autumn budget, he says. Shares are up more than 12%. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

0948 GMT - Publicis' 2025 results should be unaffected by any potential cuts to client spending against the background of tariff uncertainty, but their impact would materialize next year, CEO Arthur Sadoun says. "Whatever we could lose within the year won't have an impact before next year," Sadoun says in a call with analysts. Similarly, any additional business wins in what is left of 2025 won't have a big impact in this year's results given the time it takes to secure the account and ramp up work, he says. Shares rise 2.9% to 86.48 euros. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0946 GMT - FirstGroup's earnings estimates for FY 2025 get a boost after its performance update reinforces a strong balance sheet, RBC Capital Markets analyst Ruairi Cullinane says in a note. The transport operator's earnings per share expectations are lifted around 3% to 18.6 pence on higher anticipated variable fees in the rail division, Cullinane says. "FirstGroup offers attractions including relatively defensive credentials within the transport sector, and both a strong balance sheet and recent track record of delivery," he says. Shares are up 1.8% at 162.20 pence. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0936 GMT - Publicis Chairman and CEO Arthur Sadoun says he isn't worried about revenue falling off a cliff due to tariff uncertainty, citing new business wins that can make up for unforeseen cuts in client spending. "New business is what makes us very confident to offset potential cuts that we could not have seen at the beginning of the year," Sadoun says in a call with analysts. In a first-quarter update, the French advertising group confirmed its outlook for 2025, which calls for organic net revenue growth of 4% to 5%. Shares rise 3% to 86.52 euros. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0932 GMT - LVMH's first-quarter figures provide a negative readacross for its peers in the luxury space, Deutsche Bank analysts write in a note. The French conglomerate booked sales of 20.31 billion euros for the period, 3% down organically compared with the prior year and missing analysts' forecasts. The group noted that the main drag was Chinese demand in Japan. The lack of a dramatic slowdown in LVMH sales in the U.S. in March is somewhat reassuring given all of the noise, the analysts say. "But we suspect this may be yet to be fully reflected in the data given weaker consumer confidence," they add. Shares trade 6.9% lower at 493.75 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0929 GMT - London Stock Exchange Group shouldn't be directly affected by U.S. tariffs, JPMorgan Cazenove analysts write in a research note. While the company has a large exposure to the U.S. dollar, the majority of its revenue are recurring in nature. "A large proportion of its volume-dependent revenues can benefit from an increase in market volatility," they say. Still, LSEG has significantly underperformed other stock markets since the start of the year, partly due to perceived exposure to the U.S. market following tariff announcements, they add. Shares are up 1.2% at 112.10 pounds.(najat.kantouar@wsj.com)
